(French pronunciation: [[mɔ̃teʁeʒi]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is an administrative [region](/wiki/List_of_regions_of_Quebec "List of regions of Quebec") in the southwest part of [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"). It includes the cities of [Boucherville](/wiki/Boucherville "Boucherville"), [Brossard](/wiki/Brossard "Brossard"), [Châteauguay](/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teauguay "Châteauguay"), [Longueuil](/wiki/Longueuil "Longueuil"), [Saint-Hyacinthe](/wiki/Saint-Hyacinthe "Saint-Hyacinthe"), [Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu](/wiki/Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu "Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu"), [Salaberry-de-Valleyfield](/wiki/Salaberry-de-Valleyfield "Salaberry-de-Valleyfield") and [Vaudreuil-Dorion](/wiki/Vaudreuil-Dorion "Vaudreuil-Dorion").

**Montérégie** (French pronunciation: [[mɔ̃teʁeʒi]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is an administrative [region](/wiki/List_of_regions_of_Quebec "List of regions of Quebec") in the southwest part of [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"). It includes the cities of [Boucherville](/wiki/Boucherville "Boucherville"), [Brossard](/wiki/Brossard "Brossard"), [Châteauguay](/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teauguay "Châteauguay"), [Longueuil](/wiki/Longueuil "Longueuil"), [Saint-Hyacinthe](/wiki/Saint-Hyacinthe "Saint-Hyacinthe"), [Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu](/wiki/Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu "Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu"), [Salaberry-de-Valleyfield](/wiki/Salaberry-de-Valleyfield "Salaberry-de-Valleyfield") and [Vaudreuil-Dorion](/wiki/Vaudreuil-Dorion "Vaudreuil-Dorion").

The region had a population of 1,507,070 as of the [2016 census](/wiki/2016_Canadian_Census "2016 Canadian Census") and a land area of 11,132.34 square kilometres (4,298.22 mi2), giving it a population density of 135.4 inhabitants per square kilometre (351 inhabitants/mi2).[[1]](#cite_note-census2016-1) With approximately 18.5% of the province's population, it is the second most populous region of Quebec after [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal_(region) "Montreal (region)"). The majority of the population lives near the [Saint Lawrence River](/wiki/Saint_Lawrence_River "Saint Lawrence River"), on the south shore of [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal").

Montérégie is known for its [vineyards](/wiki/Vineyard "Vineyard"), [orchards](/wiki/Orchard "Orchard"), [maple trees](/wiki/Maple_trees "Maple trees"), panoramas, and the Monteregian mountains. The region is both [urban](/wiki/Urban_area "Urban area") (second in terms of population in Quebec) and [rural](/wiki/Rural_area "Rural area"). The regional [economy](/wiki/Economy "Economy") is based on [agriculture](/wiki/Agriculture "Agriculture") and the production of [goods and services](/wiki/Goods_and_services "Goods and services"). [Tourism](/wiki/Tourism "Tourism") also makes up a significant portion of the economy.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[Jacques Cartier](/wiki/Jacques_Cartier "Jacques Cartier") named Mont Royal in October 1535.[[3]](#cite_note-3) [Samuel de Champlain](/wiki/Samuel_de_Champlain "Samuel de Champlain") built several forts to protect the [colonists](/wiki/Settler "Settler") against the [Iroquois](/wiki/Iroquois "Iroquois") from south of the [Great Lakes](/wiki/Great_Lakes "Great Lakes"), and against the [English](/wiki/English_people "English people"), who were colonizing [New England](/wiki/New_England "New England") to the southeast.

The [toponym](/wiki/Toponymy "Toponymy") comes from the Latinized form of Mount Royal, *mons regius*. Montérégie is named for the [Monteregian Hills](/wiki/Monteregian_Hills "Monteregian Hills"), which are, in turn, named for *Mont Royal* (English: [Mount Royal](/wiki/Mount_Royal "Mount Royal")). The term for naming the set of hills in the St. Lawrence Plain was originally created in 1903 in English by geologist [Frank Dawson Adams](/wiki/Frank_Dawson_Adams "Frank Dawson Adams") to designate a new [petrographic province](/wiki/Petrographic_province "Petrographic province").

Mount Royal on [Montreal Island](/wiki/Montreal_Island "Montreal Island"), although outside the Montérégie region, is one of the Monteregian Hills. In addition to Mount Royal, two other Montérégie hills are not located in Montérégie: [Mount Mégantic](/wiki/Mount_M%C3%A9gantic "Mount Mégantic"), in [Estrie](/wiki/Estrie "Estrie"), and the hills of Oka, in the Lower Laurentians.

Montérégie was populated by the [St. Lawrence Iroquoian](/wiki/St._Lawrence_Iroquoian "St. Lawrence Iroquoian") people when the [French](/wiki/French_people "French people") began to colonize here in the early 16th century. Later colonists found their villages abandoned, and the area controlled as hunting grounds by the nations of the [Iroquoian Confederacy](/w/index.php?title=Iroquoian_Confederacy&action=edit&redlink=1 "Iroquoian Confederacy (page does not exist)") based south of the [Great Lakes](/wiki/Great_Lakes "Great Lakes").

Some of the later battles among the [European-Canadians](/wiki/European_Canadians "European Canadians") that decided the destiny of [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") took place in Montérégie. For instance, in the 1830s, [ethnic French patriots](/wiki/Lower_Canada_Rebellion "Lower Canada Rebellion") rebelled against [British government](/wiki/British_Government "British Government") troops.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The [Province of Canada](/wiki/Province_of_Canada "Province of Canada") (also called a United Canada ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Canada-Uni*)) was formed through the [Union Act in 1840](/wiki/Union_Act_of_1840 "Union Act of 1840") and February 1841, from the former provinces of [Lower Canada](/wiki/Lower_Canada "Lower Canada") and [Upper Canada](/wiki/Upper_Canada "Upper Canada").

Originally, the administrative territory of the Montérégie [parishes](/wiki/Parish "Parish") were taken from the territory of the canonical [Diocese of Saint-Jean-Longueuil](/wiki/Diocese_of_Saint-Jean-Longueuil "Diocese of Saint-Jean-Longueuil").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Subregions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Subregions")]

The Montérégie has three administrative subregions, each consisting of its own [regional county municipalities](/wiki/Regional_County_Municipalities "Regional County Municipalities") (RCM) or equivalent territories. The territorial administration of the region was conducted by three [regional conferences of elected officers](/wiki/Regional_conference_of_elected_officers "Regional conference of elected officers") ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Conférences régionales des élus*) (CRE) separate and independent in their territory:

* [Longueuil](/wiki/Agglomeration_of_Longueuil "Agglomeration of Longueuil"),
* Montérégie-Est (eastern RCMs) and
* Vallée-du-Haut-Saint-Laurent (western RCMs).

Each sub-region was organized in the same way as other administrative regions of Quebec.

### Regional county municipalities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Regional county municipalities")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b4/Carte_mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie.svg/330px-Carte_mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Carte_mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie.svg)

There are 12 [regional county municipalities](/wiki/Regional_county_municipality "Regional county municipality") and one equivalent territory in Montérégie. In 2021, [Brome-Missisquoi](/wiki/Brome-Missisquoi_Regional_County_Municipality "Brome-Missisquoi Regional County Municipality") and [La Haute-Yamaska](/wiki/La_Haute-Yamaska_Regional_County_Municipality "La Haute-Yamaska Regional County Municipality") transferred to [Estrie](/wiki/Estrie "Estrie").

#### Montérégie-Est

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Montérégie-Est")]

As of 2021, there are seven RCMs in [Montérégie Est](/wiki/Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie_Est "Montérégie Est"). Its seat is [McMasterville](/wiki/McMasterville,_Quebec "McMasterville, Quebec"), and the president is Arthur Fauteux. Its territory occupies 7,122.92 square kilometres (2,750.17 mi2) and there are 656,287 inhabitants, with a [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") of 92.1 people/km2 (239 people/mi2).

| [La Vallée-du-Richelieu](/wiki/La_Vall%C3%A9e-du-Richelieu_Regional_County_Municipality "La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality") | 124,420 | 588.60 km2 (227.26 sq mi) | 211.4 | [McMasterville](/wiki/McMasterville,_Quebec "McMasterville, Quebec") |
| [Le Haut-Richelieu](/wiki/Le_Haut-Richelieu_Regional_County_Municipality "Le Haut-Richelieu Regional County Municipality") | 117,443 | 936.02 km2 (361.40 sq mi) | 125.5 | [Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu](/wiki/Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu "Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu") |
| [Les Maskoutains](/wiki/Les_Maskoutains_Regional_County_Municipality "Les Maskoutains Regional County Municipality") | 87,099 | 1,302.90 km2 (503.05 sq mi) | 66.9 | [Saint-Hyacinthe](/wiki/Saint-Hyacinthe,_Quebec "Saint-Hyacinthe, Quebec") |
| [Marguerite-D'Youville](/wiki/Marguerite-D%27Youville_Regional_County_Municipality "Marguerite-D'Youville Regional County Municipality") *(formerly Lajammerais until January 2011)* | 77,550 | 346.04 km2 (133.61 sq mi) | 224.1 | [Verchères](/wiki/Verch%C3%A8res,_Quebec "Verchères, Quebec") |

#### Vallée-du-Haut-Saint-Laurent

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Vallée-du-Haut-Saint-Laurent")]

There are five RCMs in Vallée-du-Haut-Saint-Laurent. Its seat is [Salaberry-de-Valleyfield](/wiki/Salaberry-de-Valleyfield "Salaberry-de-Valleyfield"), and its president is Yves Daoust. Its territory occupies 3,727.22 square kilometres (1,439.09 mi2) and there are 435,436 inhabitants, with a population density of 116.8 people/km2 (303 people/mi2).

## Native Reserves

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Native Reserves")]

The population of both of these reserves are majority-[Mohawk](/wiki/Mohawk_nation "Mohawk nation"), one of the historic [Five Nations of the *Haudenosaunee*](/wiki/Iroquois#Nations "Iroquois"), or Iroquois League. [Kahnawake](/wiki/Kahnawake "Kahnawake") was established south of Montreal in 1719 as a mission village. [Akwesasne](/wiki/Akwesasne "Akwesasne") was established upriver by Mohawk leaders and their families in the mid-18th century, accompanied by French [Jesuit](/wiki/Jesuit "Jesuit") [missionaries](/wiki/Missionary "Missionary"). Akwesasne spans the boundaries of Canada and the United States, extending across the St. Lawrence River into [New York State](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), where it is referred to as the St. Regis Reservation.

* [Akwesasne](/wiki/Akwesasne "Akwesasne")
* [Kahnawake](/wiki/Kahnawake "Kahnawake")
